sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for NES port

special_mechanics.name: Knife Combat
special_mechanics.description: Omnipresent melee weapon that can be supplemented with captured arms

special_mechanics.name: Secondary Weapons
special_mechanics.description: Flamethrower (3-shot), RPG/Rocket Launcher (4-shot), Hand Grenades (3-pack). NES version removed flamethrower. In NES, each weapon pickup adds 3 uses; FDS version adds 1 use.

special_mechanics.name: Power-ups
special_mechanics.description: Star mark grants invincibility for limited time; Pistol with unlimited ammo for limited time. NES-exclusive additions.

special_mechanics.name: Climbing
special_mechanics.description: Ladder mechanics present; can climb ladders to access hidden tunnel areas (FDS version) by destroying landmines in stages 2, 4, and 5

special_mechanics.name: Respawn Mechanics
special_mechanics.description: Regional difference: Japanese FDS version respawns at death location; NES version respawns at last checkpoint (instant respawn in 2-player mode)

special_mechanics.name: Frame Canceling Exploit
special_mechanics.description: Inputting direction before attack animation finishes. NES version exhibits scrolling glitch with this technique; FDS version scrolls correctly

tier_notes: Tier 3 - Qualitative/empirical data only. No quantitative physics parameters documented in available sources. Game is primarily documented through gameplay mechanics, level design, regional differences, and regional feature variations. No ROM values, frame data, or precise movement calculations available.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Rush'n Attack (NES, Famicom Disk System)
